old_claim.note: Digital Photography (CCD/CMOS Sensors) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because CNNs are neural-network architectures for visual/spatial pattern recognition; digital camera sensors are a data-source/application context, not a prerequisite for the architecture.

source_shape.source_locator: Biological Cybernetics 1980 paper describes a neural-network model for shift-tolerant pattern recognition, not a camera-sensor technology dependency.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The scoped CNN lineage is a neural-network architecture for pattern recognition rather than a digital-imaging sensor technology.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports removing a data-acquisition/application-context edge because the model architecture can operate on patterns without depending on CCD/CMOS digital photography.

ontology_before: The graph made CNNs depend directly on digital photography sensors, conflating an application data source with the neural-network architecture.
ontology_after: The graph keeps CNNs in the neural-network architecture lineage and leaves sensor/image datasets to downstream computer-vision applications.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: direct convolutional_neural_networks -> digital_photography_ccd_cmos_sensors edge removed.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to digital-camera image classification systems rather than CNN architectures.
A downstream computer-vision dataset node requires CCD/CMOS image acquisition as a source-backed application dependency.
short_reason: Digital photography is an application data source for some CNN uses, not a prerequisite for CNN architecture.
